engineGuide.h2: Nissan GT R Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan GT R across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol
entries.history: The VR38DETT twin-turbo V6 defines the modern R35 GT-R era, delivering 478-600hp with forged internals and advanced twin-turbo technology.
compatibleVariants: R35 GT-R Premium, R35 GT-R Track Edition, R35 GT-R Black Edition, R35 GT-R 50th Anniversary Edition
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil cooling failure (CT12A/CT12B units, 60k-90k miles)
Intercooler efficiency degradation under sustained boost
Oil pump pressure loss on early R35 models (pre-2011)
entries.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETT Nissan GT R engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol Hybrid
entries.history: The VR38DETR hybrid variant powers the R35 NISMO, adding electric torque fill and enhanced cooling for track-focused performance.
compatibleVariants: R35 GT-R NISMO, R35 GT-R NISMO Special Edition
commonFailures: Hybrid system integration faults affecting ECU communication
Enhanced turbocharger wear due to higher sustained boost
Complex cooling system failures under track conditions
entries.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETR Nissan GT R engine replacement

entries.title: 3.8L Petrol Nitrous
entries.history: The VR38DETTN1 is the factory NISMO-spec engine with nitrous-ready internals and upgraded fuel delivery for extreme performance applications.
compatibleVariants: R35 GT-R NISMO, R35 GT-R Track Edition (NISMO-spec)
commonFailures: Nitrous system component wear under aggressive use

entries.title: boost turbocharger bearing failure (80k+ miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETTN1 Nissan GT R engine replacement

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: The legendary RB26DETT twin-turbo inline-6 powered all classic Skyline GT-R generations, earning iconic status for its tuning potential and racing pedigree.
compatibleVariants: R32 GT-R, R32 GT-R V-Spec, R33 GT-R, R33 GT-R V-Spec, R34 GT-R, R34 GT-R V-Spec, R34 GT-R V-Spec II, R34 GT-R M-Spec, R34 GT-R M-Spec Nur
commonFailures: Head gasket failure due to narrow coolant passages (Nissan SB 97-NT-01, 70k-100k miles)
Oil pump drive collar wear causing catastrophic oil pressure loss (early R32, 60k+ miles)
Timing chain tensioner wear leading to chain slap and potential interference
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB26DETT Nissan GT R engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: The RB25DET turbo inline-6 appeared in early R32 GT-R variants before the RB26DETT became standard, offering strong performance with simpler maintenance.
commonFailures: Fuel pump pressure loss after 80k miles
Turbocharger oil seal degradation under high boost
Timing belt wear requiring preventative replacement at 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB25DET Nissan GT R engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The RB20DET turbo inline-6 powered very early R32 GT-R test mules and limited export variants, representing the foundation of Nissan's RB performance engine family.
compatibleVariants: R32 GT-R Early Export Variants (limited UK availability)
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear under sustained boost
Timing belt tensioner failure requiring preventative replacement
Fuel injector clogging affecting idle quality and power delivery
entries.cta: Get quotes for RB20DET Nissan GT R engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan GT R Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: RB26DETT Head Gasket Failure - Why Classic GT-R Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan GT R R32, R33, R34 (1989-2002, RB26DETT)
problems.rootCause: Narrow coolant passages in the Y33 platform architecture cause localized overheating, leading to head gasket degradation as documented in Nissan Service Bulletin 97-NT-01.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Head Gasket Replacement - Replace gasket before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace OEM gasket with upgraded ARP studs, pressure test cooling system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents major damage but doesn't address root coolant flow design

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete RB26DETT rebuild with upgraded internals
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded head gaskets, forged internals, updated cooling components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; addresses root cause with modern components, suitable for high-mileage or track use

problems.recommendation: On an R32/R33/R34 GT-R valued at £30,000-£80,000+, a full rebuilt engine with upgraded head gaskets offers better long-term value than repeated preventative repairs. We recommend the rebuilt option if mileage exceeds 80k or if coolant loss symptoms are present.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt RB26DETT engine prices for your Nissan GT R

problems.h4: VR38DETT Turbocharger Failure - Why R35 GT-R Engines Lose Boost
problems.rootCause: CT12A/CT12B turbocharger units suffer oil cooling system limitations under sustained high-boost operation, leading to bearing wear, oil coking, and eventual turbo failure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbocharger Rebuild/Replacement - Replace failed turbos only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and replace CT12A/CT12B turbos with rebuilt or upgraded units, update oil cooling lines, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate failure but doesn't prevent recurrence if oil cooling root cause isn't resolved

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete VR38DETT rebuild with turbo upgrades
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded turbochargers, enhanced oil cooling, updated intercooler components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for high-mileage R35 or track-focused owners; prevents repeat turbo failure with system-wide upgrades

problems.recommendation: For R35 GT-R owners experiencing turbo-related symptoms (smoking, loss of boost, oil consumption), a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbos and oil cooling offers superior reliability versus standalone turbo replacement, especially beyond 70k miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt VR38DETT engine prices for your Nissan GT R

problems.h4: RB26DETT Oil Pump Drive Failure - Catastrophic R32 Engine Risk
problems.affectedModels: Nissan GT R R32 (1989-1994, RB26DETT early production)
problems.rootCause: The oil pump drive collar (crank collar) wears prematurely on early R32 engines, causing oil pump disengagement and catastrophic oil pressure loss leading to complete engine seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Oil Pump Collar Upgrade - Replace collar before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove oil pump, replace worn collar with upgraded billet component, pressure test oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended preventative measure for early R32 owners; prevents catastrophic failure if done before symptoms appear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete RB26DETT rebuild with upgraded oil system
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded oil pump, billet collar, enhanced baffles, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if engine has already suffered oil pressure loss; addresses root cause with modern components

problems.recommendation: Early R32 GT-R owners should proactively upgrade the oil pump collar at 60k miles regardless of symptoms. If oil pressure warning lights have appeared, assume internal damage has occurred and opt for a full rebuilt engine.
problems.cta: Get quotes for RB26DETT oil pump upgrade or replacement for your Nissan GT R

problems.h4: VR38DETT Intercooler Efficiency Loss - R35 Heat Soak Issues
problems.rootCause: Factory intercooler cores degrade under repeated thermal cycling and high boost pressures, reducing charge cooling efficiency and causing power loss, especially during track use or hot weather.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Intercooler Replacement/Upgrade - Replace core only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove factory intercooler, fit upgraded core with improved flow characteristics, update piping if required
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses immediate heat soak but may not resolve underlying cooling system limitations on heavily modified vehicles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete VR38DETT rebuild with enhanced cooling
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded intercooler, enhanced oil/water cooling, updated turbo components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for track-focused R35 owners; prevents repeat heat-related failures with comprehensive cooling upgrades

problems.recommendation: For street-driven R35 GT-Rs showing mild heat soak, an intercooler upgrade offers good value. For track use or heavily modified vehicles, a rebuilt engine with comprehensive cooling upgrades provides superior long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare VR38DETT cooling system upgrade options for your Nissan GT R

problems.h4: RB26DETT Timing Chain Wear - High-Rev Engine Maintenance
problems.affectedModels: Nissan GT R R32, R33, R34 (1989-2002, RB26DETT)
problems.rootCause: The RB26DETT's high-revving nature accelerates timing chain and tensioner wear, leading to chain stretch, rattling noises, and potential valve timing misalignment if neglected.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Timing System Service - Replace chain, guides, tensioners
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain, guides, tensioners with OEM-spec components, verify timing alignment
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative maintenance that extends engine life; recommended at 80k miles regardless of symptoms

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete RB26DETT rebuild with updated timing components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, forged internals, enhanced oiling, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines or those showing advanced wear; addresses multiple failure points simultaneously

problems.recommendation: Proactive timing system service at 80k miles is cost-effective preventative maintenance. If chain rattle or timing-related codes are present, assess overall engine condition before deciding between service and full rebuild.
problems.cta: Get quotes for RB26DETT timing system service or replacement for your Nissan GT R

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan GT R
finalCta.paragraph: The Nissan GT R's performance pedigree demands specialist attention when engine issues arise. Whether you're battling RB26DETT head gasket failure on a classic R32/R33/R34 or VR38DETT turbo issues on a modern R35, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand GT-R-specific failure patterns. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original weakness, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices from trusted specialists and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan GT R Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan GT R and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Nissan GT R Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Nissan GT-R's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The R32 GT-R introduced the legendary RB26DETT twin-turbo inline-6, establishing the GT-R's performance icon status with ATTESA-ETS all-wheel drive.
keyChanges: Introduction of RB26DETT 2.6L twin-turbo inline-6 engine
ATTESA-ETS Pro all-wheel drive system debut
Super HICAS four-wheel steering on V-Spec models
Early R32 models featured RB25DET before RB26DETT standardisation
knownFor: RB26DETT head gasket vulnerability (Nissan SB 97-NT-01)
Oil pump drive collar wear on early production engines
Legendary tuning potential and Group A racing heritage
High collector values driving specialist rebuild demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 1989-1994 Nissan GT R replacement engines
years.ticker: RB26DETT head gasket replacement • R32 oil pump collar upgrade • RB26DETT timing chain service

years.description: The R33 GT-R refined the RB26DETT platform with improved chassis dynamics and enhanced cooling, though head gasket concerns persisted across the generation.
keyChanges: RB26DETT received minor cooling system refinements
ATTESA-ETS Pro system updated for improved traction
Body structure stiffened for enhanced handling precision
V-Spec models gained Brembo brakes and upgraded suspension
knownFor: Continued RB26DETT head gasket vulnerability requiring upgraded gaskets
Improved chassis balance over R32 but heavier curb weight
Strong aftermarket support for performance modifications
Growing collector interest driving replacement engine demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995-1998 Nissan GT R replacement engines
years.ticker: R33 RB26DETT rebuild • RB26 head gasket upgrade • R33 timing system service

years.preview: R34 GT-R Iconic Final RB26 Generation
years.description: The R34 GT-R represented the pinnacle of RB26DETT development with advanced electronics, though the fundamental head gasket design limitation remained unaddressed.
keyChanges: RB26DETT received final refinements with improved ECU mapping
Multi-Function Display (MFD) introduced for real-time performance monitoring
V-Spec II models gained active LSD and enhanced aerodynamics
Final production year (2002) marked end of RB26DETT era
mainEngines: RB26DETT - 2.6L petrol twin-turbo (GT-R, V-Spec, V-Spec II, M-Spec Nur)
knownFor: Most sought-after RB26DETT application with highest collector values
Persistent head gasket failure risk requiring preventative upgrades
Advanced electronics increasing specialist diagnostic requirements
Strong enthusiast community driving high-quality rebuild demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 1999-2002 Nissan GT R replacement engines
years.ticker: R34 RB26DETT head gasket • R34 V-Spec II engine rebuild • RB26 oil pump upgrade

years.description: The R35 GT-R introduced the all-new VR38DETT twin-turbo V6, marking a complete architectural shift from the RB26DETT with advanced technology and unprecedented performance.
keyChanges: Introduction of VR38DETT 3.8L twin-turbo V6 engine
Dual-clutch GR6 transmission replacing manual gearbox
Advanced ATTESA-ETS Pro with active torque vectoring
Early R35 models (2007-2010) featured 478hp output
mainEngines: VR38DETT - 3.8L petrol twin-turbo V6 (Premium, Black Edition)
knownFor: VR38DETT turbocharger oil cooling issues on CT12A units
Early R35 oil pump concerns requiring monitoring
Groundbreaking performance redefining supercar expectations
High replacement costs driving specialist rebuild market growth
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2014 Nissan GT R replacement engines
years.ticker: VR38DETT turbo replacement • R35 oil pump service • VR38 intercooler upgrade

years.preview: R35 GT-R Matures with Performance Variants
years.description: Mid-cycle R35 updates brought power increases to 565hp, Track Edition variants, and refined VR38DETT components addressing early-generation concerns.
keyChanges: VR38DETT power increased to 565hp with updated turbochargers
Track Edition variant introduced with enhanced cooling and suspension
CT12B turbochargers replaced CT12A units with improved reliability
Interior and infotainment systems received significant updates
mainEngines: VR38DETT - 3.8L petrol twin-turbo V6 (Premium, Track Edition)
knownFor: Improved VR38DETT reliability with CT12B turbochargers
Track Edition cooling upgrades addressing heat soak concerns
Continued high performance maintaining supercar competitiveness
Growing mileage on early R35 driving replacement engine enquiries
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2020 Nissan GT R replacement engines
years.ticker: R35 Track Edition engine • VR38DETT CT12B turbo • R35 50th Anniversary rebuild

years.description: The R35 NISMO introduced the VR38DETR hybrid system and ultimate performance specification, representing the most complex GT-R powertrain to date.
keyChanges: Introduction of VR38DETR hybrid variant for NISMO models
Power output increased to 600hp with electric torque fill
Enhanced cooling systems for track-focused applications
Final production updates maintaining R35 competitiveness
popularModels: Nissan R35 GT-R NISMO Special Edition
knownFor: VR38DETR hybrid system complexity requiring specialist expertise
Ultimate factory GT-R performance with 600hp capability
Limited production numbers driving premium replacement pricing
Advanced diagnostics essential for hybrid system integration

years.ticker: R35 NISMO hybrid engine • VR38DETR specialist rebuild • NISMO turbo upgrade

faq.h2: Nissan GT R Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What engine does a Nissan GT R have?
items.answer: The Nissan GT-R uses twin-turbo petrol engines only: the iconic RB26DETT 2.6L inline-6 in R32/R33/R34 models (1989-2002) and the modern VR38DETT 3.8L V6 in R35 models (2007-present).
keyPoints: RB26DETT powers classic Skyline GT-R generations with legendary tuning potential
VR38DETT delivers 478-600hp in the contemporary R35 supercar
No diesel or hybrid options exist outside the NISMO VR38DETR variant
Engine code verification via registration ensures correct replacement selection
items.cta: Compare Nissan GT R engine codes and prices for your variant

items.question: How much does a Nissan GT R VR38DETT engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt VR38DETT engines for the R35 GT-R typically cost £12,000-£15,000 supply-only in the UK, with NISMO variants reaching £14,000-£17,000 due to hybrid system complexity.
keyPoints: Prices reflect specialist rebuilds addressing turbocharger oil cooling issues
Supply & fit installation adds £1,500-£3,500 depending on workshop location
All rebuilt units include upgraded components and 12-month warranty
Compare multiple specialist quotes to secure best value for your R35
items.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETT Nissan GT R engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: Is the Nissan GT R engine reliable?
items.answer: GT-R engines are robust when properly maintained, but RB26DETT units suffer head gasket failures and VR38DETT engines experience turbocharger issues at higher mileage without preventative upgrades.
keyPoints: RB26DETT head gasket vulnerability addressed via Nissan SB 97-NT-01 upgrades
VR38DETT turbocharger oil cooling requires monitoring beyond 60k miles
Regular servicing and specialist diagnostics extend engine lifespan significantly
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components offer improved long-term reliability
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Nissan GT R engine with upgraded reliability components

items.question: What causes RB26DETT head gasket failure?
items.answer: Narrow coolant passages in the RB26DETT cylinder head cause localized overheating, leading to gasket degradation as documented in Nissan Service Bulletin 97-NT-01.
keyPoints: Failure typically occurs between 70,000-100,000 miles on classic GT-R models
Upgraded ARP studs and improved gaskets prevent recurrence during rebuilds
Coolant loss or overheating symptoms require immediate specialist assessment
Preventative upgrades recommended at 60k miles for early R32 owners
items.cta: Compare rebuilt RB26DETT engines with head gasket upgrades for your Nissan GT R

items.question: What causes VR38DETT turbocharger failure?
items.answer: CT12A/CT12B turbocharger units on the VR38DETT suffer oil cooling system limitations under sustained high-boost operation, leading to bearing wear and eventual failure.
keyPoints: Turbo issues commonly appear between 60,000-90,000 miles on R35 GT-R models
Smoking exhaust, loss of boost or oil consumption indicate turbo degradation
Rebuilt engines with upgraded turbos and enhanced oil cooling prevent recurrence
Specialist diagnostics essential for accurate turbo system assessment
items.cta: Get quotes for VR38DETT turbo replacement or full engine rebuild for your R35

items.question: How much does a Nissan GT R engine rebuild cost UK?
items.answer: RB26DETT rebuilds cost £8,000-£11,000 while VR38DETT rebuilds range £12,000-£15,000 supply-only in the UK, with NISMO variants commanding premium pricing.
keyPoints: Prices include upgraded internals addressing known failure patterns
Supply & fit installation adds £1,500-£3,500 depending on workshop
All rebuilt engines carry minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Compare multiple specialist quotes to secure best value for your variant
items.cta: Compare Nissan GT R engine rebuild prices from trusted UK specialists

items.question: Will an RB26DETT fit an R35 GT-R?
items.answer: No, the RB26DETT and VR38DETT are completely different engines with incompatible mounting points, electronics and transmission interfaces; generation-specific replacements are essential.
keyPoints: RB26DETT is a 2.6L inline-6; VR38DETT is a 3.8L V6 with different architecture
ECU, wiring harness and transmission systems are generation-specific
Attempting cross-generation swaps requires extensive fabrication and is not recommended
Always verify engine code matches your GT-R generation before ordering replacement
items.cta: Find the correct engine code for your Nissan GT R generation via registration lookup

items.question: How long does a Nissan GT R engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, RB26DETT engines can exceed 120,000 miles while VR38DETT units often reach 100,000+ miles, though known failure points require preventative attention.
keyPoints: RB26DETT head gasket and oil pump issues require monitoring beyond 70k miles
VR38DETT turbocharger and cooling system need assessment after 60k miles
Regular specialist servicing and quality fluids significantly extend engine life
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components offer enhanced longevity versus original specification
items.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt Nissan GT R engine with extended longevity components

items.question: Where can I buy a Nissan GT R engine in the UK?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects UK GT-R owners with vetted specialists offering rebuilt, reconditioned and used RB26DETT and VR38DETT engines with nationwide delivery and warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Specialists understand GT-R-specific failure patterns and upgrade requirements
All engines undergo bench testing and include minimum 12-month warranty
Supply-only or full supply & fit options available depending on your needs
Compare multiple quotes to secure best value and specialist expertise for your variant
items.cta: Compare Nissan GT R engine prices from trusted UK specialists today

items.question: Can I rebuild my Nissan GT R RB26DETT engine?
items.answer: Yes, RB26DETT engines are excellent candidates for rebuilds with upgraded head gaskets, forged internals and enhanced oiling systems addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: Rebuilds typically cost £8,000-£11,000 supply-only with upgraded components
ARP studs, improved gaskets and billet oil pump collars prevent repeat failures
Specialist rebuilders familiar with RB26 architecture ensure proper assembly
Rebuilt engines offer better value than repeated preventative repairs on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for an RB26DETT rebuild with upgraded internals for your Nissan GT R
